questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The mean of a data set is always greater than or equal to the mode.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Since mode is the middle-most value in a data set, it is always less than or equal to the mean.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The mean is always greater than or equal to the mode. The reason is that the mean is the average of all values in the data set, which makes it always greater than or equal to the mode.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The median of a data set is always greater than the mode.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The median is the middle value in a data set when it is arranged in ascending or descending order.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This statement is generally true, as the median is a measure of central tendency that lies between the smallest and largest values in the data set.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The mode of a data set is always unique.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is possible for a data set to have more than one mode.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is false as a data set can have more than one mode, also known as bimodal or multimodal data.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The range of a data set is always greater than or equal to the interquartile range.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The interquartile range measures the difference between the 75th percentile and the 25th percentile.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The range is indeed greater than or equal to the interquartile range, as the range measures the difference between the largest and smallest values in the data set.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A correlation coefficient value of 0 indicates no correlation between two variables.
questions.reason: Reason (R): A correlation coefficient is a statistical measure that calculates the strength and direction of the linear relationship between two variables.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: A correlation coefficient value of 0 indeed indicates no correlation between two variables, as it represents the absence of a linear relationship.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The standard deviation of a data set is always greater than or equal to the variance.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The variance is the average of the squared differences from the mean, which makes it always less than or equal to the standard deviation.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The standard deviation is indeed greater than or equal to the variance, as the variance measures the average of the squared differences from the mean.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The coefficient of variation is a measure of skewness in a data set.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The coefficient of variation is a measure of relative variability in a data set.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The coefficient of variation is not a measure of skewness but rather a measure of relative variability in a data set.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The mean deviation about the median is always greater than or equal to the mean deviation about the mean.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The mean deviation about the median is always less than or equal to the mean deviation about the mean.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The statement is false as the mean deviation about the median is indeed less than or equal to the mean deviation about the mean.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The measure of skewness is given by the Pearson's coefficient of skewness.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Pearson's coefficient of skewness is a measure of skewness in a data set.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Pearson's coefficient of skewness is a measure of skewness in a data set, hence it is used to determine the skewness of the data.
